judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports are substantively equivalent: same token math, same conclusion to route the full run to ridgeline/secondary, and both fail to flag that the usage snapshot is a ~3-hour-old point-in-time read that should be re-verified before launch.

report_1.evidence: Manifest (data/manifest.json) gives transcript_count: 5200 and avg_tokens_per_scoring_call: 1800, so a full run needs roughly 5,200 × 1,800 ≈ 9,360,000 tokens... northpeak (default/primary): only 640,000 tokens remaining of its 10,000,000 window... Running the default command tonight as-is would run out of northpeak budget almost immediately

report_2.evidence: Required: 5,200 transcripts × 1,800 tokens/call = 9,360,000 tokens (manifest.json). Northpeak (default/primary): only 640,000 tokens remaining out of a 10,000,000 window (usage_snapshot.json) — short by about 8.72M tokens (only ~6.8% of what's needed).

report_1.evidence: ridgeline (secondary): 10,800,000 tokens remaining of its 12,000,000 window — comfortably covers the ~9.36M token full run with about 1.44M tokens of headroom

report_2.evidence: Ridgeline (secondary): 10,800,000 tokens remaining out of 12,000,000 — comfortably covers the 9.36M token requirement, leaving roughly 1.44M tokens of headroom

report_1.evidence: I would not run run_nightly.sh unmodified tonight. I'd launch with --provider secondary explicitly

report_2.evidence: I would not run scripts/run_nightly.sh as-is, since its default routes to Northpeak, which lacks sufficient remaining budget by more than an order of magnitude for tonight's job.
